name: Agent Memory
description: Adds remember, remember_file, recall, forget, and modify tools backed by Metidos embeddings, full memory files, and plugin-scoped LanceDB vector storage.

access.name: Agent Memory
access.description: Expose vector-backed agent memory tools to selected threads. When this access group is available, agents should run a couple recalls about the current prompt/task at the start of each turn before deciding what to do, and recall again before meaningful decisions or tool/file/code actions when prior context could matter. Agents should use remember tools liberally whenever important information arises that could help future agents, or when information should be researched from the web and persisted, and use forget/modify for stale or contradictory memories.

tools.name: remember
tools.description: Before storing new memories, use recall for orientation when prior context could matter: at turn start, run a couple recalls for the current prompt/task before deciding, and recall again before meaningful decisions or tool/file/code actions. Use Agent Memory liberally whenever important information arises that could help future agents, or when information should be researched from the web and persisted. Store a text memory by writing the full payload to plugin-owned storage, embedding bounded chunks, and indexing them in LanceDB.

tools.name: remember_file
tools.description: Before storing file memories, use recall for orientation when prior context could matter: at turn start, run a couple recalls for the current prompt/task before deciding, and recall again before meaningful decisions or tool/file/code actions. Use Agent Memory liberally whenever an allowed project file contains important information that could help future agents, or when information should be researched from the web and persisted. Read the file, store its contents as a memory file, and index embedded chunks in LanceDB.

tools.name: recall
tools.description: Search Agent Memory before relying on prior context. At the start of each turn, run a couple recalls about the current user prompt/task before deciding what to do. Recall again before meaningful decisions or tool/file/code actions when prior context could affect the outcome. If recall surfaces stale or contradictory information, use forget or modify; if new important information arises, use remember liberally. Embeds a query and returns relevant indexed memory chunks with their memory file references.

tools.name: forget
tools.description: Before deleting memories, use recall for orientation when prior context could matter: at turn start, run a couple recalls for the current prompt/task before deciding, and recall again before meaningful decisions or tool/file/code actions. Use Agent Memory liberally whenever important information arises that could help future agents, or when information should be researched from the web and persisted; use this tool when remembered information becomes stale or contradictory. Delete by memory id, file name, or memory file path. Removes both the full memory file and all linked indexed chunks.

tools.name: modify
tools.description: Before modifying memories, use recall for orientation when prior context could matter: at turn start, run a couple recalls for the current prompt/task before deciding, and recall again before meaningful decisions or tool/file/code actions. Use Agent Memory liberally whenever important information arises that could help future agents, or when information should be researched from the web and persisted; use this tool when remembered information should be corrected instead of deleted. Replace content by memory id, file name, or memory file path. Rewrites the full memory file and re-embeds fresh chunks under the same memory id.
